description: Column extraction re-test (same as Phase 10 methodology)

description: --resize 1120 (downscale image to 1120px wide)

description: Error analysis run — systematic review of all failures

description: --known-names flag + name normalisation (fuzzy merge post-process)

notes: 71.4%; neutral — llama fills x not times; no improvement

description: 5+2 votes + ISO date keys (combined experiment)
notes: 56.0%; REGRESSED -11.5 pts; BOTH changes harmful — violated one-variable rule

description: 5+2 votes only (extra voting runs at temp=0)
notes: 57.7%; REGRESSED — extra votes lock in errors at temp=0, no diversity — NEVER ADD VOTING RUNS AT TEMP=0

description: True baseline established (3+2 votes, day-names, known-names)

description: Numbered-column prompt + drift detector
notes: 62.7% avg; REGRESSED — high variance; numbered columns -3.4 pts

description: Drift detector only, temp=0.1

description: temperature 0.1 → 0.0 (fully deterministic)

description: Blank fallback improvements (conditional re-query guard)
notes: +0.6 pct pts = +1 shift; conditional re-query guard

description: Drift detector threshold=1 + ExtractRowAsync integration
notes: 61.3%; REVERTED — threshold=1 fires on any employee off day 1 (normal schedule)

description: Quality guards: CountValidShifts defensive checks
notes: 69.6%; defensive commit — prevents future regressions; no score change

description: Phase 33 OCR garbage sanitize confirmed with --known-names

description: Anti-shift warning in ExtractColumnAsync only (isolated)

description: 1 row + 4 col vote reweight
notes: 40.5%; CATASTROPHIC — temp=0 produces 4 identical wrong copies; vote reweighting useless at temp=0

description: CRITICAL anti-WRONG-COL warning moved to TOP of rules

description: Anti-WRONG-COL warning in ExtractRowAsync
notes: 78.0%; neutral — fallback path not exercised in normal runs

notes: 64.3%; 75 min runtime; bigger model WORSE with CPU offload — NEVER use CPU offload

notes: +20 shifts — LARGEST SINGLE GAIN

notes: 89.9% (151/168) 2-image all-time best with --known-names; +13 shifts

description: IM(3) added to test set (3 images, 252 shifts total)
notes: 75.4% (190/252); IM(3) Sep21-27 2025, 12 employees, 84 shifts; LLM returns only 7-10 of 12 employees for IM(3)

description: Session name pool + late OCR name supplement (cross-image name accumulation)
notes: +20 shifts; Andee/Brittney/Cyndee/Sarah/Franny recovered in IM(3); OCR fuzzy-match adds missing employees with empty shifts

description: OCR fragments before pass 2 + non-orthogonal coordinate hint in all prompts

description: Last-employee re-query when output array length = n-1 (truncation fix)
notes: +1 shift; Ciara Oct31 fixed

description: Last-employee re-query extended to blank-last case
notes: +1 shift; Ciara Oct29 fixed

description: Penultimate duplicate-value re-query (Seena Oct29)
notes: 230/252 (91.3%) all-time 3-image best; +1 shift; Seena Oct29 fixed

description: Adjacent-duplicate re-query (re-ask for any two consecutive identical values in column)
notes: REVERTED — false positives on legitimate shared values (Halle/Kyleigh both work 2:00-6:30 Oct26)

description: Triplet detection — any value appearing >=3 times in a column triggers re-query
notes: REVERTED — triggered on RTO/PTO holiday columns (IM(2) Nov27 Thanksgiving) — NEVER use count-in-column heuristics for RTO/PTO

description: Triplet detection restricted to TimeRangeRegex matches only
notes: REVERTED — fires correctly on Thu Oct30 but re-query returns same answer at temp=0 (highlighted strip visual anchor persists)

description: IM(4) and IM(5) added to test set (5 images, 434 shifts total); architectural fixes to uniform-RTO and holidayBlankedDayIndices
notes: 86.2% (374/434); IM(4) Jul27-Aug2, IM(5) Jul20-26 2025, 13 employees each

description: Retry result header stripping (retryResult for uniform-RTO was not having day-name/ISO header stripped)
notes: 86.2%; neutral score; architectural correctness fix

description: 8:xx start-time contamination fix — narrow strip re-query when digit from hours sub-column bleeds in
notes: +3 shifts; IM(4) Thu fixed; digit contamination from hours sub-column — narrow crop re-query

description: Name parenthetical normalization (Athena(train) → Athena)
notes: 377/434 neutral in benchmark (non-deterministic LLM name variants); committed for correctness

description: Leading-RTO contamination re-query (re-ask when first 3 employees all return RTO)
notes: 372/434 (85.7%); REVERTED — false positives: first-3-RTO pattern appears on legitimate columns too; indistinguishable from contamination at temp=0

rationale: Shifting the left edge of each day-column strip crop inward by 6 pixels avoids including the grid line or trailing pixels from the adjacent column, which was causing systematic row misalignment in the LLM's spatial parsing of the Thu Oct30 strip.

search_preview: "Each cell contains: a time range (e.g. 9:00-5:30), RTO, PTO
rationale: Adding an explicit instruction that time ranges must include both start and end times separated by a dash prevents the model from truncating ranges like '9:00-5:30' to just '9:00' or misreading them, targeting the TIME-MISREAD errors for Jenny, Kyleigh, Brittney, and Tori.

search_preview: "Each cell contains: a time range (e.g. 9:00-5:30), RTO, PTO
rationale: Adding an explicit instruction that time ranges must always include both start and end times separated by a dash prevents the model from outputting truncated ranges like '9:00' instead of '9:00-5:30', targeting the TIME-MISREAD errors for Jenny, Kyleigh, Brittney, and Tori.

search_preview: "Each cell contains: a time range (e.g. 9:00-5:30), RTO, PTO
rationale: Adding an explicit instruction that time ranges must always include both start AND end times separated by a dash prevents the model from outputting truncated ranges like '9:30-6:00' when the actual value is '9:30-2:00', targeting TIME-MISREAD errors for Jenny, Kyleigh, Brittney, Tori, and Halle.

search_preview: "Each cell contains: a time range (e.g. 9:00-5:30), RTO, PTO
rationale: Adding an explicit instruction that time ranges must always include both start AND end times with a dash prevents the model from truncating ranges (e.g. outputting '9:30-6:00' when the actual value is '9:30-2:00'), targeting TIME-MISREAD errors for Jenny, Kyleigh, Brittney, Tori, and Halle.

search_preview: "Each cell contains: a time range (e.g. 9:00-5:30), RTO, PTO
rationale: Adding an explicit instruction that time ranges must include both start AND end times with a dash, emphasizing accurate end-time reading, targets the TIME-MISREAD errors where the model outputs incorrect end times (e.g. '9:30-6:00' instead of '9:30-2:00' for Kyleigh, '10:30-6:00' instead of '9:30-6:30' for Jenny).

search_preview: "The top row of the RIGHT column is a HEADER showing the day
rationale: Adding an explicit instruction that time ranges must include both start AND end times, with emphasis on reading the end time carefully, targets the TIME-MISREAD errors where the model outputs wrong end times (e.g. '9:30-6:00' instead of '9:30-2:00' for Kyleigh, '10:30-6:00' instead of '9:30-6:30' for Jenny).

search_preview: "Each cell contains: a time range (e.g. 9:00-5:30), RTO, PTO
rationale: Adding an explicit instruction to read the end time carefully and emphasizing that both start and end times must be read accurately targets the TIME-MISREAD errors where the model outputs wrong end times (e.g. '9:30-6:00' instead of '9:30-2:00' for Kyleigh, '10:30-6:00' instead of '9:30-6:30' for Jenny).

search_preview: "Each cell contains: a time range (e.g. 9:00-5:30), RTO, PTO
rationale: Adding explicit instructions that time ranges must include both start AND end times with a dash, and to read both carefully, targets the TIME-MISREAD errors where the model outputs truncated or wrong ranges (e.g. '9:30-6:00' instead of '9:30-2:00' for Kyleigh, '10:30-6:00' instead of '9:30-6:30' for Jenny).

search_preview: Read ONLY the employee rows (not the header) in the RIGHT co
rationale: Ciara (the last employee) is consistently getting blank results because the LLM stops reading before the final row; explicitly instructing the model to output a value for every employee including the last one should prevent the off-by-one row dropout causing the WRONG_BLANK errors for Ciara on Oct29, Oct31, Nov01.

search_preview: Read ONLY the employee rows (not the header) in the RIGHT co
rationale: Ciara (last employee) consistently gets blank results because the LLM stops before outputting the final row; adding an explicit instruction that every employee including the last must have a value in the output array should fix the 3 WRONG_BLANK errors for Ciara on Oct29, Oct31, Nov01.

search_preview: "Each cell contains: a time range (e.g. 9:00-5:30), RTO, PTO
rationale: Adds explicit instructions that (1) time ranges must include both start and end times (fixing TIME-MISREAD errors for Jenny/Kyleigh/Brittney/Tori/Halle) and (2) the output array must have exactly N entries including the last employee by name (fixing WRONG_BLANK errors for Ciara on Oct29/Oct31/Nov01).

description: 80% majority check for both uniform-RTO (inner) and holiday detector (outer) — instead of strict allSame
rationale: IM(5) Jul26 Sat: LLM returns 12/13 RTO + Seena 2:30-8:00. Strict allSame fails on Seena outlier, so neither uniform-RTO re-query nor holiday detector fire. Proposed fix: 80% majority mode check for both.
notes: Hard ceiling confirmed. Root cause: narrow retry also returns varied-but-no-time-ranges for Jul26. When holiday detector fires (80% fix), Jenny+Kyleigh become blank (wrong) -2, Seena becomes correct +1, net -1. OCR salvage can only recover 2 cells (Victor=RTO, Seena=RTO). IM(5) Jul26 Sat is confirmed hard ceiling: model pattern-completes all-RTO for Sat column, OCR cannot read time-range values in that column.

description: Holiday-guard discriminator: skip holiday blank when ocrTimeMap has ≥1 time-range in the column (store-open signal)
rationale: IM(4) Aug2 Sat: model returns all-RTO for a normal work day → holiday detector false-fires → 9 employees with time-range shifts become blank. Hypothesis: if WinRT OCR found time-range tokens in the column, the store is open and we should skip the holiday blank.
notes: Diagnostic run shows ocrTimeMap has 0 time-range(s) for ALL 7 day columns of IM(4). WinRT OCR scanned 163 elements but none matched TimeRangeRegex (time cells read as fragments 9:00 + 5:30 separately, not combined 9:00-5:30). Guard never fires — dead code for this dataset. IM(4) Aug2 Sat confirmed hard ceiling: same pattern as IM(5) Jul26 — model pattern-completes all-RTO for Sat, OCR cannot read time-range values from this image.

description: Retail-context hint (v3) for Sat/Sun columns in ExtractColumnFromImageAsync: injected at START of both initial prompt and reinforcedPrompt retry
rationale: Model has a strong Saturday=day-off prior. Previous v1/v2 hints targeted OllamaCalendarService.ExtractColumnAsync which is dead code in the hybrid pipeline. Real call sites are HybridCalendarService.ExtractColumnFromImageAsync initial prompt and reinforcedPrompt retry. v3 adds CRITICAL warning to the initial prompt and a STOP message to the reinforcedPrompt.
notes: IM(4): 78->86/91 (+8). IM(5): 70->79/91 (+9). IM(2): 78->77/91 (-1). IM(1)+IM(3) unchanged. reinforcedRetailHint in the retry broke through the prior. narrow-reinforced retry now reports time-ranges for Sat columns.
